issue.title: Factor pyvision into symvision and migrate sase + chezmoi
Phase 1 — Create the repo and port the tool (code + dev tooling)
issue.description: Work in ~/projects/github/bbugyi200/symvision/ (create it in this phase as the sibling checkout specified by the plan). Create the public repository, scaffold packaging and development tooling, port pyvision to symvision, add smoke tests, and verify checks plus behavior parity.

issue.title: Phase 2 — Port the regression suite to pytest + coverage
issue.description: Work in ~/projects/github/bbugyi200/symvision/. Port all 24 chezmoi bash regression tests to pytest, add focused unit coverage, enforce the highest honest coverage threshold (target at least 90%), and verify just check.

issue.title: Phase 3 — CI, release automation, README
issue.description: Work in ~/projects/github/bbugyi200/symvision/. Add CI and release workflows, release-please configuration, the GitHub pypi environment, and comprehensive README documentation; verify CI and leave the v0.1.0 release PR open for Phase 4.

issue.title: Phase 4 — First release: v0.1.0 on PyPI
issue.description: Work in ~/projects/github/bbugyi200/symvision/. USER CHECKPOINT: before this phase runs, Bryan must register the symvision pending trusted publisher on pypi.org for bbugyi200/symvision, publish.yml, environment pypi. Verify the publisher or stop with a clear notification; then merge the release PR, watch publishing, and independently verify symvision 0.1.0.

issue.title: Phase 5 — Migrate sase to the published package
issue.description: Work in the sase repo/workspace and run just install first. Replace the vendored pyvision integration with symvision>=0.1.0,<0.2.0, rename pragmas and live references, update tests/config/docs, and verify just symvision plus just check. Memory files, AGENTS.md, and provider shims require explicit user permission in this phase agent's own conversation; otherwise leave them untouched and flag the follow-up.

issue.title: Phase 6 — Retire the chezmoi script
issue.description: Work in the chezmoi repo opened via sase workspace open. Delete the pyvision script and regression suite, clean remaining live pyvision references while preserving archived plans and other vendored tools, run chezmoi's just test, and commit through the sanctioned workflow.

fields.notes: Created public github.com/bbugyi200/symvision with master as default; ported pyvision into focused typed modules with symvision naming, hatchling/uv tooling, self-dogfooding, toobig checks, and four CLI smoke tests. just check passes. Real SASE scratch-tree parity run produced identical normalized diagnostics and matching exit status. Pushed feat commit 34bf0fa.

Ported all 24 chezmoi bash regressions to pytest with shared temporary git/external-repo fixtures; added focused CLI, scanner, pragma, entry-point, external ordering/cache, and fake BD_COMMAND coverage; enforced fail_under=94; just check passes with 67 tests and 94.27% coverage.

Release PR #1 merged as e865044f66dc1cc21909b7938d6f002e26cfd9ee. Publish run 29211186259: release, build, twine validation, and install-smoke passed; PyPI publish failed with invalid-publisher (valid token, but no corresponding publisher). Register pending trusted publisher: project symvision, owner bbugyi200, repository symvision, workflow publish.yml, environment pypi; then rerun failed jobs and independently verify PyPI + clean uv tool install. Phase intentionally remains in_progress.

Migration implemented in the sase workspace: added symvision>=0.1.0,<0.2.0 dev dependency; replaced pyvision Justfile recipes/stages with the published symvision CLI; renamed live source pragmas/comments and non-protected docs/config references; updated recipe/CI tests; deleted tools/pyvision-260708. Protected memory/*.md, AGENTS.md, and provider shims were intentionally untouched because this conversation did not grant explicit permission. Verification against GitHub tag v0.1.0 (e865044): just symvision clean; focused tests 12 passed; full just test 16742 passed, 7 skipped; just check passed formatting and every lint stage including symvision, then stopped at a pre-existing init-memory freshness failure reproduced on clean origin/master. BLOCKED: just install cannot resolve symvision from PyPI because sase-5t.4 publishing failed invalid-publisher; Bryan must register the trusted publisher and rerun the publish job, then this phase must rerun registry-based just install and just check before closing.

Migration is implemented and rebased as commit 39451d036. Added symvision>=0.1.0,<0.2.0; replaced vendored pyvision recipes/stages with symvision; preserved the upstream sase-5u(get_max_running_agents) epic allowance in both private and public recipes; renamed live pragmas/comments and non-protected docs/config references; updated tests; deleted tools/pyvision-260708. Protected memory/*.md, AGENTS.md, and provider shims remain untouched because this conversation did not explicitly authorize those files. Verification: exact v0.1.0 GitHub tag e865044 installs and reports 0.1.0; just symvision passes; focused tests 12 passed; formatting and all lint stages in just check pass. Full just test: 16755 passed, 7 skipped, with one unrelated pre-existing runner-slot/Rust binding failure in tests/test_core_agent_scan_options.py. just check stops at the pre-existing init-memory freshness failure. BLOCKED: PyPI JSON returns 404 and registry-based just install remains unsatisfiable because phase sase-5t.4 is still in_progress after invalid-publisher. Register the PyPI trusted publisher and complete sase-5t.4, then rerun registry-based just install and just check before closing this bead.

Release PR #1 merged as e865044f66dc1cc21909b7938d6f002e26cfd9ee. The PyPI trusted publisher was registered, and rerunning the failed jobs in Publish run 29211186259 succeeded. PyPI reports symvision 0.1.0, and a clean 'uv tool install symvision' produced a working CLI. Phase verified complete.

Registry-based migration verification completed in the sase workspace: 'just install' resolved 98 packages with --no-sources and installed the project successfully, including symvision>=0.1.0,<0.2.0 from the registry; 'just symvision' passed cleanly against src/sase with the preserved sase-5u(get_max_running_agents) allowance. 'just check' passed all formatting and lint gates, then stopped only at the known pre-existing init-memory freshness gate for memory/README.md and provider shims; those edits are explicitly out of scope. Running the test gate separately passed 16,756 tests with 7 skipped.

payload.close_reason: All six phases verified complete; symvision 0.1.0 is published and independently installable, sase uses the registry package with clean Symvision lint and full tests, and chezmoi legacy pyvision files are removed.

fields.notes:
COMMIT: 2903fa9

Recovery reopened 2026-07-13: the prior epic closure cited only the companion-repository bookkeeping commit and had treated verification from an unlanded numbered-workspace migration as completion. The SASE consumer migration is now durably present on origin/master as commit 039204fe2, but the explicitly authorized protected memory/instruction migration remains to be implemented and landed before final closure.

Registry verification and the consumer migration were previously performed from ephemeral workspace state, so the earlier closure did not prove a landed SASE change. Recovery baseline on 2026-07-13 confirms the consumer migration is now durably reachable from origin/master as commit 039204fe2. Phase reopened to complete the explicitly authorized memory/pyvision.md -> memory/symvision.md migration, regenerate protected instructions through the sanctioned workflow, and record verification. Do not close until all recovery changes are durably landed.

Registry verification and the consumer migration were previously performed from ephemeral workspace state, so the earlier closure did not prove a landed SASE change. Recovery baseline on 2026-07-13 confirms the consumer migration is durably reachable from origin/master as commit 039204fe2.

Recovery implementation completed in the current workspace with user-authorized protected-memory edits: memory/pyvision.md was replaced by memory/symvision.md; root and tools agent instructions, generated memory index, and provider shims were refreshed through sase memory init --no-commit; the public symvision recipe now delegates to _lint-symvision; regression coverage prevents restoration of _lint-pyvision or tools/pyvision-*.

Verification: just install resolved symvision==0.1.0 from the registry; just symvision passed; focused Justfile/CI tests passed (14 passed); sase memory init --check passed and provider shims match their AGENTS.md sources; the live-surface audit has no pyvision references outside intentional negative regression assertions; just test passed (16,787 passed, 7 skipped). just check passed Python/Markdown formatting and all lint stages (keep-sorted, ruff, mypy, pyscripts, symvision, toobig), then stopped at SASE validation only because the pre-existing plans companion needs generated SDD README/map refresh; memory init and sdd validate themselves are current/passing, and no recovery change touches SDD state.

Keep Phase 5 and epic sase-5t open until these uncommitted recovery edits have a durable SASE commit and ChangeSpec/PR reachable from origin/master.

COMMIT: 2903fa9

Recovery reopened 2026-07-13: the prior epic closure cited only the companion-repository bookkeeping commit and had treated verification from an unlanded numbered-workspace migration as completion. The SASE consumer migration is durably present on origin/master as commit 039204fe2. The user-authorized protected memory/instruction recovery is now implemented and verified in the current workspace, with detailed results on sase-5t.5, but remains uncommitted and unlanded. Keep this epic open until the recovery changes have a durable SASE commit and ChangeSpec/PR reachable from origin/master.
